topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a jarring sound in the otherwise quiet afternoon. A name flashed on the screen: 'Jax_The_Legend'. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. I stared at it, my thumb hovering over the 'Accept' button like it was a live wire. Jax. The name tasted like old chalk dust and sharp edges. I felt a tightening in my chest, a knot forming just beneath my ribs. Why now? After all this time, after I’d built a life that felt solid, this ghost from my past decided to reappear. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in my small apartment. I paced the worn rug, my jaw clenched so tightly my molars ached. I needed to delete it, block it, erase the digital tether before I lost control of the entire situation.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, the glass cool against my palm. The profile picture loaded: a smirk that used to make my stomach churn. It was him. The sheer audacity of it made my breath catch. I watched the little blue circle indicating a pending request, and a wave of sheer annoyance washed over me, leaving a sour taste in my mouth. I scrolled through my contacts, trying to find a logical explanation, a reason why this connection was necessary. There was none. It was just an unwelcome intrusion, a reminder of years spent navigating sharp corners and pointed words. I threw the phone onto the sofa cushion, the movement sharp and jerky. I stood up, walked to the window, and stared out at the street, wishing the glass would crack just to release the pressure building behind my eyes.

[story 3]

The email notification popped up, demanding my attention. It was a friend request. My hands began to tremble slightly as I read the username. It was him. The memory of his sneering face, the way he used to dismantle my confidence with casual cruelty, flooded my senses. A low growl started in my throat, a sound I immediately tried to swallow down. I leaned back in my chair, pressing the heels of my hands against my temples, trying to physically push the unwelcome feeling away. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to spring, but I remained still, trapped by the sheer weight of the encounter. I took a deep, shaky breath, the air feeling thin in my lungs, and reluctantly tapped the screen, the action feeling heavy and momentous.
